vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-712: fix stale pan work/cloister/specialists refs in .claude/skills/

plan.title: fix: stale pan work/cloister/specialists refs in .claude/skills/
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-6

narratives.Problem: Project-level skill files under .claude/skills/ still reference the pre-PAN-705 command taxonomy (pan work <verb>, pan cloister, pan specialists). These references mislead any agent that loads the affected skills about correct CLI syntax. A grep audit found 5 stale occurrences in exactly 2 files: .claude/skills/test-specialist-workflow/SKILL.md (4 refs) and .claude/skills/update-panopticon-docs/resources/EXAMPLES.md (1 ref).
narratives.Proposal: Two trivial, independent doc edits — one bead per file. Bead 1 rewrites the test-specialist-workflow skill to use `pan admin cloister start` and `pan admin specialists wake ...`. Bead 2 rewrites the pan-work example in the update-panopticon-docs skill to reflect the flat lifecycle taxonomy (no `pan work` namespace). A final anchored grep (\bpan (work|cloister|specialists)\b) over .claude/skills/ must return zero hits; `pan workspace` references are intentionally preserved because that command group still exists post-PAN-705.

items.title: Update test-specialist-workflow SKILL.md to pan admin cloister/specialists

narrative.Action: Edit .claude/skills/test-specialist-workflow/SKILL.md. Replace `pan cloister start` (line 40) with `pan admin cloister start`. Replace the three `pan specialists wake <agent>` lines (163, 164, 165) and the `pan specialists wake test-agent --task "..."` line (174) with their `pan admin specialists wake ...` equivalents. Do not change any other content. Verify by running `pan admin cloister --help` and `pan admin specialists --help` against the resulting command strings.

subItems.title: File .claude/skills/test-specialist-workflow/SKILL.md contains zero occurrences of `pan cloister` or `pan specialists` (as standalone commands, not `pan admin cloister`/`pan admin specialists`)
Every rewritten command in the file is a valid subcommand of `pan admin cloister` or `pan admin specialists` as shown by `pan admin cloister --help` and `pan admin specialists --help`
No unrelated changes in the file (diff is limited to the 5 stale command references and minimal surrounding whitespace)

items.title: Update update-panopticon-docs EXAMPLES.md Pattern 6 to drop pan work namespace

narrative.Action: Edit .claude/skills/update-panopticon-docs/resources/EXAMPLES.md. Replace the `pan work --help` verify step (line 294) with `pan --help` (or a more specific command like `pan start --help` if the surrounding example is about the `start` verb). Review Pattern 6's surrounding prose and update any wording that still implies a `pan work` command namespace — after PAN-705, lifecycle verbs (`start`, `done`, `kill`, `recover`, `tell`, `resume`, `approve`, `close`, `reopen`, `sync-main`, `wipe`) are top-level, not subcommands of `pan work`. Keep the example's original teaching intent intact.

subItems.title: File .claude/skills/update-panopticon-docs/resources/EXAMPLES.md contains zero occurrences of `pan work ` (trailing space, to exclude `pan workspace`)
Pattern 6 prose and example commands are consistent with the post-PAN-705 flat lifecycle taxonomy — no text implies lifecycle verbs live under a `pan work` group

Repo-wide audit `grep -rn -E '\bpan (work|cloister|specialists)\b' .claude/skills/` returns zero hits across all files under .claude/skills/ (this is the issue-level acceptance criterion and must be re-run after both beads land)
